---
title: "Audit Target: xrf"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Executive Summary

The data quality audit of the XRF elemental analysis dataset, comprising 2,431 records across 24 biomass resources, reveals a significant shift in data distribution compared to the reference set. The most prominent issue is the high frequency of zero values (8.1% of the 'observed_value' column), which were virtually absent in the reference data. These zeros likely represent values below the instrument's Limit of Detection (LOD) rather than true absences, particularly for trace elements like Lanthanum (La) and Neodymium (Nd). The dataset is characterized by high variance in macronutrients (K, Ca) and significant outliers in markers for soil contamination (Si, Ti, Fe) and heavy metals (Th, U), especially within almond-derived feedstocks.

The audit identified two primary providers: 'tiny' and 'capstan'. Data attributed to analyst xkang2@lbl.gov (provider 'tiny') demonstrates higher volatility in trace elements for almond hulls, including unexpected detections of Uranium and Thorium. Conversely, records from jduong@lbl.gov (provider 'capstan') show anomalies in almond shells for elements like Copper (Cu) and Praseodymium (Pr). The total absence of descriptive metadata in the 'note' and 'analyst_name' fields limits the ability to contextualize these outliers, suggesting a need for improved data entry protocols during the XRF measurement and reporting phase.

## Evidently Profile

<iframe src="../evidently/xrf.html" width="100%" height="800px" frameborder="0"></iframe>

## Flagged Observations

| Record ID | Resource | Provider | Sample Date | Parameter | Value | Z-Score | Severity |
